objective: Find explicit direct Manipulation label sources without counting unlabeled or rule-only sources as accepted labels.

sources.name: Mendeley Data: Detecting Crypto Wash Trades via Machine Learning
sources.reason: Public page describes trade rows with a binary legitimate/wash label; must re-fetch current files and rerun unchanged chronological support, coverage, ECE, and positive/negative control gates before acceptance.

sources.name: GitHub: nft_wash_trading
sources.reason: Related repository may explain feature/label construction, but code alone is not an accepted label panel.

sources.name: Dune nft.wash_trades
sources.reason: Curated wash-trade table is relevant, but it must be exported with timestamps, positive/negative windows, and replayable provenance before calibration.

sources.name: Bitquery wash-trading detector label module
sources.reason: Rule-generated labels are sidecar/provenance only until validated against accepted source windows.

sources.name: NFT Wash Trading Detection paper
sources.reason: Definition and method provenance only; no Board A source-label panel was materialized.

why_not_accepted: No source was exported or re-audited into a chronological positive/negative label window panel in this run.
Mendeley v3 is a candidate, not accepted evidence until unchanged gates pass.
Dune requires export and replayable provenance before calibration.
Bitquery rule labels and papers are not independent accepted labels by themselves.

next_action: Run a bounded Mendeley v3 re-fetch/re-audit first; if it still fails, try a Dune nft.wash_trades export path. Do not count Bitquery rule labels or paper definitions as accepted labels without independent validation.
